🎨 Aesthetic Design — Phase 1
Load an existing brief, generate a new one, approve, or delete. Run against a campaign slug.

Hero Slogan
Two Tiny Windows, 35mm at Sea

Point‑and‑shoot ease. One swap. Sail on.

Voice Persona
A calm print lover who knows good window light and respects the sea.

Casting Goal
Across the set: relaxed pairs and solos first, with a few small trios—people in sea-day ease who happen to love film and small print swaps.

Age Range Guidance
Core 25–45 with meaningful presence of 46–65; occasional early‑20s and 65+ to keep it real to cruise life.

Diversity Intent
Visible mix of skin tones and backgrounds (Black, Asian, Latine, White, mixed). Include varied body types, hairstyles, and personal style. If head coverings or cultural dress appear, treat them as natural wardrobe—not theme markers.

Pairing Guidance
Vary pairings: two friends, mixed‑age duo, queer couple, solo traveler at the rail, small trio at a café. Avoid repeating one default couple profile across shots.

Anti-Stereotype Rules
• No retro-costume cosplay to signal ‘film’
• No gatekeeper ‘pro gear’ casting—anti‑elitist by design
• No all‑white, all‑20‑something ensembles
• No performative influencer poses; keep it conversational and relaxed
• No suggestive or adult-themed zine art in public spaces
• Don’t center lanyards, pins, or props as identity crutches—people first
Community Core Promise
Meet fellow film people in passing, trade one tiny keepsake, and still keep your day wide open.

Participation Style
Entirely drop‑in: arrive during a loose window, make a single swap or quick portrait, or just say hi and drift on—no RSVP, no list, no set times or hosts.

Social Gravity
Little cues—a stitched cuff mark, a pocket strap, a matte mini‑print—spark rail‑side chats and café smiles without needing a plan.

Copy Framing Rule
Use opt‑in phrasing: ‘If you like,’ ‘Drop by for a minute,’ ‘One quick trade,’ never obligations or schedules. Add privacy: share your own photos; avoid filming others. Recognition in walkways is verbal or a nod and under five seconds. Phone photo sharing happens once seated or at adjacent seating only.

Optional Gatherings
• If you notice a tiny coffee mini‑print/postcard swap at café window seating one afternoon, join for a minute (indoors, seated). If it’s busy, choose a quiet lounge corner nearby.
• Shaded‑promenade pairs later in the day: a quick opt‑in portrait in passing—keep right, keep strolling.
• Indoor‑only micro‑stamping during a seated coffee swap; then tuck it away.
Red-Team Summary
Exceptionally mature, ship‑first, opt‑in aesthetic with clear anti‑program guardrails, privacy norms, diverse casting intent, and thorough production ops. However, two location mismatches on Rhapsody (Windjammer “open‑air terrace” and Café Latte‑tudes presented as ocean‑windowed with curved hull glass) create cruise‑implausible scenes. Several “lean” phrases undercut strict rail/balcony posture safety, and minor typos could confuse crew. These must be corrected before launch.

block
Approval Recommendation
Hold for revision. Replace/retarget the two implausible venues to confirmed Rhapsody spaces and unify all rail/balcony posture language; then clean typos and proceed to re‑review.

Required Fixes
• Replace SC7 Windjammer “open‑air terrace (aft)” with a confirmed Rhapsody‑accurate venue and update all related text/prompts.
• Verify Café Latte‑tudes sightlines; if no ocean view, retarget coffee‑swap visuals to a verified ocean‑window venue and remove “curved hull/ocean‑through‑glass” language tied to Latte‑tudes.
• Unify rail/balcony safety language by removing all “lean” phrasing and replacing with “forearms resting lightly, torso upright, hands inside rail line” wherever rails appear (SC3, LS1, LS4, hero_explainer notes).
• Resolve typos/duplications/punctuation artifacts across sceneLibrary, storyboards, and countdown notes for a clean production handoff.
Issues
blocker
cruise implausibility
Windjammer “open‑air terrace (aft)” likely not present on Rhapsody of the Seas
productionBible.sceneLibrary.SC7: "Windjammer Café open‑air terrace (aft, dining area)" with terrace rail and outdoor breeze described; Rhapsody’s Windjammer is windowed but typically lacks an open‑air aft terrace.

Replace SC7 with a verified Rhapsody location offering similar light and ocean context (e.g., indoor Windjammer window seating, Solarium/Schooner Bar windows, or shaded pool‑deck seating under overhang). Update scene text, prompts, and any storyboard/still references to an “open‑air terrace.”

blocker
cruise implausibility
Café Latte‑tudes depicted with ocean‑facing curved glass is likely inaccurate on Rhapsody
SC2/LS2/LS6 describe “Café Latte‑tudes window seating,” “curved ship‑hull window glass,” and ocean drift through glass while noting Centrum adjacency; on Rhapsody, Latte‑tudes is Centrum‑side and may not have ocean‑facing curved windows.

Site‑verify Latte‑tudes sightlines. If no ocean view, retarget coffee‑swap visuals to a confirmed ocean‑window venue (e.g., Schooner Bar window tables, Viking Crown Lounge daytime window seating, or other verified Deck‑side windows). Replace all “curved hull/ocean through glass” mentions tied to Latte‑tudes.

warning
motion safety
Ambiguous “lean” phrasing conflicts with strict rail/balcony posture rules
SC3 imagePrompt: “a traveler leans into the view”; hero_explainer shot 1: “relaxed lean”; LS1 subjectAction: “leaning into the breeze”; LS4: “lean shoulder‑to‑shoulder toward the wake.” Global rules elsewhere require upright torso, hands inside rail line, no leaning over/sitting.

Standardize to: “forearms resting lightly, torso upright, hands inside rail line” for all deck and balcony rails. Replace all “lean/leans into” language in SC3, LS1, LS4, and hero_explainer shot notes. Add an explicit balcony posture note mirroring deck‑rail safety in visual.compositionNotes.

warning
production feasibility
Typos/template artifacts may cause on‑set confusion
SC8 location: “Oceanview stateroom stateroom desk with ocean‑view ” (duplicate word/trailing space); hero_explainer shot 4: “sslow handheld…”; TikTok storyboard shot 3: “jogging gimbal follow curve”; multiple double periods in countdownSeries visualDirectionNotes.

Proof and correct all duplicated words, misspellings, and punctuation across sceneLibrary, storyboards, and countdown notes before handoff.

warning
copy alignment
Ringer tee text risks reading like operations signage if foregrounded
Merch niche item: Unisex Ringer Tee front text “Keep Right, Single‑File.” Prominent use in hero frames could imply instruction/queueing, conflicting with the no‑program promise.

Do not feature this tee in hero/CTA placements or as a readable close‑up. If shown at all, keep it incidental and pair with opt‑in, ambient copy to avoid signage vibes.

Generated: 3/15/2026, 10:15:15 PM
By: agent

Several “lean” phrases undercut strict rail/balcony posture safety, and minor typos could confuse crew. These must be corrected before launch.",

      "Detailed production bible with small crew, off‑peak capture, and movement constraints",

    ],

Interactive Agent Dev
🎨 Aesthetic Design — Phase 1
Load an existing brief, generate a new one, approve, or delete. Run against a campaign slug.

Campaign Slug
film-and-zine-afloat-2026
Load
Revision deadlock after 2 cycles. The same class of issues survived multiple revisions. Operator intervention required. Surviving fixes: Replace SC7 Windjammer “open‑air terrace (aft)” with a confirmed Rhapsody‑accurate venue and update all related text/prompts.; Verify Café Latte‑tudes sightlines; if no ocean view, retarget coffee‑swap visuals to a verified ocean‑window venue and remove “curved hull/ocean‑through‑glass” language tied to Latte‑tudes.; Unify rail/balcony safety language by removing all “lean” phrasing and replacing with “forearms resting lightly, torso upright, hands inside rail line” wherever rails appear (SC3, LS1, LS4, hero_explainer notes).; Resolve typos/duplications/punctuation artifacts across sceneLibrary, storyboards, and countdown notes for a clean production handoff.
Deterministic Fix Available
These structural issues can be fixed without re-running the LLM. Select the fixes to apply, preview, then apply.

textOnLight
#1F2329
Hero Slogan
Two Tiny Windows, 35mm at Sea

Point‑and‑shoot ease. One swap. Sail on.

Voice Persona
A calm print lover who knows good window light and respects the sea.

Casting Goal
Across the set: relaxed pairs and solos first, with a few small trios—people in sea-day ease who happen to love film and small print swaps.

Age Range Guidance
Core 25–45 with meaningful presence of 46–65; occasional early‑20s and 65+ to keep it real to cruise life.

Diversity Intent
Visible mix of skin tones and backgrounds (Black, Asian, Latine, White, mixed). Include varied body types, hairstyles, and personal style. If head coverings or cultural dress appear, treat them as natural wardrobe—not theme markers.

Pairing Guidance
Vary pairings: two friends, mixed‑age duo, queer couple, solo traveler at the rail, small trio at a café. Avoid repeating one default couple profile across shots.

Anti-Stereotype Rules
• No retro-costume cosplay to signal ‘film’
• No gatekeeper ‘pro gear’ casting—anti‑elitist by design
• No all‑white, all‑20‑something ensembles
• No performative influencer poses; keep it conversational and relaxed
• No suggestive or adult-themed zine art in public spaces
• Don’t center lanyards, pins, or props as identity crutches—people first
Community Core Promise
Meet fellow film people in passing, trade one tiny keepsake, and still keep your day wide open.

Participation Style
Entirely drop‑in: arrive during a loose window, make a single swap or quick portrait, or just say hi and drift on—no RSVP, no list, no set times or hosts.

Social Gravity
Little cues—a stitched cuff mark, a pocket strap, a matte mini‑print—spark rail‑side chats and café smiles without needing a plan.

Copy Framing Rule
Use opt‑in phrasing: ‘If you like,’ ‘Drop by for a minute,’ ‘One quick trade,’ never obligations or schedules. Add privacy: share your own photos; avoid filming others. Recognition in walkways is verbal or a nod and under five seconds. Phone photo sharing happens once seated or at adjacent seating only.

Optional Gatherings
• If you notice a tiny coffee mini‑print/postcard swap at café window seating one afternoon, join for a minute (indoors, seated). If it’s busy, choose a quiet lounge corner nearby.
• Shaded‑promenade pairs later in the day: a quick opt‑in portrait in passing—keep right, keep strolling.
• Indoor‑only micro‑stamping during a seated coffee swap; then tuck it away.
Red-Team Summary
Exceptionally mature, ship‑first, opt‑in aesthetic with clear anti‑program guardrails, privacy norms, diverse casting intent, and thorough production ops. However, two location mismatches on Rhapsody (Windjammer “open‑air terrace” and Café Latte‑tudes presented as ocean‑windowed with curved hull glass) create cruise‑implausible scenes. Several “lean” phrases undercut strict rail/balcony posture safety, and minor typos could confuse crew. These must be corrected before launch.

block
Approval Recommendation
Hold for revision. Replace/retarget the two implausible venues to confirmed Rhapsody spaces and unify all rail/balcony posture language; then clean typos and proceed to re‑review.

Required Fixes
• Replace SC7 Windjammer “open‑air terrace (aft)” with a confirmed Rhapsody‑accurate venue and update all related text/prompts.
• Verify Café Latte‑tudes sightlines; if no ocean view, retarget coffee‑swap visuals to a verified ocean‑window venue and remove “curved hull/ocean‑through‑glass” language tied to Latte‑tudes.
• Unify rail/balcony safety language by removing all “lean” phrasing and replacing with “forearms resting lightly, torso upright, hands inside rail line” wherever rails appear (SC3, LS1, LS4, hero_explainer notes).
• Resolve typos/duplications/punctuation artifacts across sceneLibrary, storyboards, and countdown notes for a clean production handoff.
Issues
blocker
cruise implausibility
Windjammer “open‑air terrace (aft)” likely not present on Rhapsody of the Seas
productionBible.sceneLibrary.SC7: "Windjammer Café open‑air terrace (aft, dining area)" with terrace rail and outdoor breeze described; Rhapsody’s Windjammer is windowed but typically lacks an open‑air aft terrace.

Replace SC7 with a verified Rhapsody location offering similar light and ocean context (e.g., indoor Windjammer window seating, Solarium/Schooner Bar windows, or shaded pool‑deck seating under overhang). Update scene text, prompts, and any storyboard/still references to an “open‑air terrace.”

blocker
cruise implausibility
Café Latte‑tudes depicted with ocean‑facing curved glass is likely inaccurate on Rhapsody
SC2/LS2/LS6 describe “Café Latte‑tudes window seating,” “curved ship‑hull window glass,” and ocean drift through glass while noting Centrum adjacency; on Rhapsody, Latte‑tudes is Centrum‑side and may not have ocean‑facing curved windows.

Site‑verify Latte‑tudes sightlines. If no ocean view, retarget coffee‑swap visuals to a confirmed ocean‑window venue (e.g., Schooner Bar window tables, Viking Crown Lounge daytime window seating, or other verified Deck‑side windows). Replace all “curved hull/ocean through glass” mentions tied to Latte‑tudes.

warning
motion safety
Ambiguous “lean” phrasing conflicts with strict rail/balcony posture rules
SC3 imagePrompt: “a traveler leans into the view”; hero_explainer shot 1: “relaxed lean”; LS1 subjectAction: “leaning into the breeze”; LS4: “lean shoulder‑to‑shoulder toward the wake.” Global rules elsewhere require upright torso, hands inside rail line, no leaning over/sitting.

Standardize to: “forearms resting lightly, torso upright, hands inside rail line” for all deck and balcony rails. Replace all “lean/leans into” language in SC3, LS1, LS4, and hero_explainer shot notes. Add an explicit balcony posture note mirroring deck‑rail safety in visual.compositionNotes.

warning
production feasibility
Typos/template artifacts may cause on‑set confusion
SC8 location: “Oceanview stateroom stateroom desk with ocean‑view ” (duplicate word/trailing space); hero_explainer shot 4: “sslow handheld…”; TikTok storyboard shot 3: “jogging gimbal follow curve”; multiple double periods in countdownSeries visualDirectionNotes.

Proof and correct all duplicated words, misspellings, and punctuation across sceneLibrary, storyboards, and countdown notes before handoff.

warning
copy alignment
Ringer tee text risks reading like operations signage if foregrounded
Merch niche item: Unisex Ringer Tee front text “Keep Right, Single‑File.” Prominent use in hero frames could imply instruction/queueing, conflicting with the no‑program promise.

Do not feature this tee in hero/CTA placements or as a readable close‑up. If shown at all, keep it incidental and pair with opt‑in, ambient copy to avoid signage vibes.

Generated: 3/15/2026, 10:15:15 PM
By: agent

Niche identity stays pocket‑sized—wardrobe stitch, slim strap, tiny zine—never a gear parade. People appear as relaxed solos or pairs; natural diversity across ages and styles. The ocean and ship architecture lead every frame; humans are calm accents. Off‑ship Nassau shot keeps the ship clearly in frame to avoid resort generic. 🧪 Test Lab
Interactive Agent Dev
🎨 Aesthetic Design — Phase 1
Load an existing brief, generate a new one, approve, or delete. Run against a campaign slug.

Campaign Slug
film-and-zine-afloat-2026
Load
Selected deterministic fixes were already satisfied in the current brief. The stale fix panel was cleared. Re-run Red Team or Revise to generate fresh suggestions.
Actions
Re-generate
Re-gen Bible
Re-run Red Team
Revise
Approve
Delete Brief
Identity Summary
revision #2
revised
red-team block
Color Palette
primary
#0E2A47
secondary
#9CCFC6
accent
#E96E5A
background
#F7F3EA
textOnDark
#F2F0E6
textOnLight
#1F2329
Hero Slogan
Two Tiny Windows, 35mm at Sea

Point‑and‑shoot ease. One swap. Sail on.

Voice Persona
A calm print lover who knows good window light and respects the sea.

Casting Goal
Across the set: relaxed pairs and solos first, with a few small trios—people in sea-day ease who happen to love film and small print swaps.

Age Range Guidance
Core 25–45 with meaningful presence of 46–65; occasional early‑20s and 65+ to keep it real to cruise life.

Diversity Intent
Visible mix of skin tones and backgrounds (Black, Asian, Latine, White, mixed). Include varied body types, hairstyles, and personal style. If head coverings or cultural dress appear, treat them as natural wardrobe—not theme markers.

Pairing Guidance
Vary pairings: two friends, mixed‑age duo, queer couple, solo traveler at the rail, small trio at a café. Avoid repeating one default couple profile across shots.

Anti-Stereotype Rules
• No retro-costume cosplay to signal ‘film’
• No gatekeeper ‘pro gear’ casting—anti‑elitist by design
• No all‑white, all‑20‑something ensembles
• No performative influencer poses; keep it conversational and relaxed
• No suggestive or adult-themed zine art in public spaces
• Don’t center lanyards, pins, or props as identity crutches—people first
Community Core Promise
Meet fellow film people in passing, trade one tiny keepsake, and still keep your day wide open.

Participation Style
Entirely drop‑in: arrive during a loose window, make a single swap or quick portrait, or just say hi and drift on—no RSVP, no list, no set times or hosts.

Social Gravity
Little cues—a stitched cuff mark, a pocket strap, a matte mini‑print—spark rail‑side chats and café smiles without needing a plan.

Copy Framing Rule
Use opt‑in phrasing: ‘If you like,’ ‘Drop by for a minute,’ ‘One quick trade,’ never obligations or schedules. Add privacy: share your own photos; avoid filming others. Recognition in walkways is verbal or a nod and under five seconds. Phone photo sharing happens once seated or at adjacent seating only.

Optional Gatherings
• If you notice a tiny coffee mini‑print/postcard swap at café window seating one afternoon, join for a minute (indoors, seated). If it’s busy, choose a quiet lounge corner nearby.
• Shaded‑promenade pairs later in the day: a quick opt‑in portrait in passing—keep right, keep strolling.
• Indoor‑only micro‑stamping during a seated coffee swap; then tuck it away.
Red-Team Summary
Exceptionally mature, ship‑first, opt‑in aesthetic with clear anti‑program guardrails, privacy norms, diverse casting intent, and thorough production ops. However, two location mismatches on Rhapsody (Windjammer “open‑air terrace” and Café Latte‑tudes presented as ocean‑windowed with curved hull glass) create cruise‑implausible scenes. Several “lean” phrases undercut strict rail/balcony posture safety, and minor typos could confuse crew. These must be corrected before launch.

block
Approval Recommendation
Hold for revision. Replace/retarget the two implausible venues to confirmed Rhapsody spaces and unify all rail/balcony posture language; then clean typos and proceed to re‑review.

Required Fixes
• Replace SC7 Windjammer “open‑air terrace (aft)” with a confirmed Rhapsody‑accurate venue and update all related text/prompts.
• Verify Café Latte‑tudes sightlines; if no ocean view, retarget coffee‑swap visuals to a verified ocean‑window venue and remove “curved hull/ocean‑through‑glass” language tied to Latte‑tudes.
• Unify rail/balcony safety language by removing all “lean” phrasing and replacing with “forearms resting lightly, torso upright, hands inside rail line” wherever rails appear (SC3, LS1, LS4, hero_explainer notes).
• Resolve typos/duplications/punctuation artifacts across sceneLibrary, storyboards, and countdown notes for a clean production handoff.
Issues
blocker
cruise implausibility
Windjammer “open‑air terrace (aft)” likely not present on Rhapsody of the Seas
productionBible.sceneLibrary.SC7: "Windjammer Café open‑air terrace (aft, dining area)" with terrace rail and outdoor breeze described; Rhapsody’s Windjammer is windowed but typically lacks an open‑air aft terrace.

Replace SC7 with a verified Rhapsody location offering similar light and ocean context (e.g., indoor Windjammer window seating, Solarium/Schooner Bar windows, or shaded pool‑deck seating under overhang). Update scene text, prompts, and any storyboard/still references to an “open‑air terrace.”

blocker
cruise implausibility
Café Latte‑tudes depicted with ocean‑facing curved glass is likely inaccurate on Rhapsody
SC2/LS2/LS6 describe “Café Latte‑tudes window seating,” “curved ship‑hull window glass,” and ocean drift through glass while noting Centrum adjacency; on Rhapsody, Latte‑tudes is Centrum‑side and may not have ocean‑facing curved windows.

Site‑verify Latte‑tudes sightlines. If no ocean view, retarget coffee‑swap visuals to a confirmed ocean‑window venue (e.g., Schooner Bar window tables, Viking Crown Lounge daytime window seating, or other verified Deck‑side windows). Replace all “curved hull/ocean through glass” mentions tied to Latte‑tudes.

warning
motion safety
Ambiguous “lean” phrasing conflicts with strict rail/balcony posture rules
SC3 imagePrompt: “a traveler leans into the view”; hero_explainer shot 1: “relaxed lean”; LS1 subjectAction: “leaning into the breeze”; LS4: “lean shoulder‑to‑shoulder toward the wake.” Global rules elsewhere require upright torso, hands inside rail line, no leaning over/sitting.

Standardize to: “forearms resting lightly, torso upright, hands inside rail line” for all deck and balcony rails. Replace all “lean/leans into” language in SC3, LS1, LS4, and hero_explainer shot notes. Add an explicit balcony posture note mirroring deck‑rail safety in visual.compositionNotes.

warning
production feasibility
Typos/template artifacts may cause on‑set confusion
SC8 location: “Oceanview stateroom stateroom desk with ocean‑view ” (duplicate word/trailing space); hero_explainer shot 4: “sslow handheld…”; TikTok storyboard shot 3: “jogging gimbal follow curve”; multiple double periods in countdownSeries visualDirectionNotes.

Proof and correct all duplicated words, misspellings, and punctuation across sceneLibrary, storyboards, and countdown notes before handoff.

warning
copy alignment
Ringer tee text risks reading like operations signage if foregrounded
Merch niche item: Unisex Ringer Tee front text “Keep Right, Single‑File.” Prominent use in hero frames could imply instruction/queueing, conflicting with the no‑program promise.

Do not feature this tee in hero/CTA placements or as a readable close‑up. If shown at all, keep it incidental and pair with opt‑in, ambient copy to avoid signage vibes.

Niche identity stays pocket‑sized—wardrobe stitch, slim strap, tiny zine—never a gear parade. People appear as relaxed solos or pairs; natural diversity across ages and styles. The ocean and ship architecture lead every frame; humans are calm accents. Off‑ship Nassau shot keeps the ship clearly in frame to avoid resort generic. No tracks, cranes, sliders, or floor cables in passenger walkways. Use handheld or compact gimbal only. No exchanges, greetings, or handoffs on gangways or in primary embark/disembark flow paths. 🧪 Test Lab
Interactive Agent Dev
🎨 Aesthetic Design — Phase 1
Load an existing brief, generate a new one, approve, or delete. Run against a campaign slug.

Campaign Slug
film-and-zine-afloat-2026
Load
Actions
Re-generate
Re-gen Bible
Re-run Red Team
Revise
Approve
Delete Brief
Identity Summary
revision #2
revised
red-team block
Color Palette
primary
#0E2A47
secondary
#9CCFC6
accent
#E96E5A
background
#F7F3EA
textOnDark
#F2F0E6
textOnLight
#1F2329
Hero Slogan
Two Tiny Windows, 35mm at Sea

Point‑and‑shoot ease. One swap. Sail on.

Voice Persona
A calm print lover who knows good window light and respects the sea.

Casting Goal
Across the set: relaxed pairs and solos first, with a few small trios—people in sea-day ease who happen to love film and small print swaps.

Age Range Guidance
Core 25–45 with meaningful presence of 46–65; occasional early‑20s and 65+ to keep it real to cruise life.

Diversity Intent
Visible mix of skin tones and backgrounds (Black, Asian, Latine, White, mixed). Include varied body types, hairstyles, and personal style. If head coverings or cultural dress appear, treat them as natural wardrobe—not theme markers.

Pairing Guidance
Vary pairings: two friends, mixed‑age duo, queer couple, solo traveler at the rail, small trio at a café. Avoid repeating one default couple profile across shots.

Anti-Stereotype Rules
• No retro-costume cosplay to signal ‘film’
• No gatekeeper ‘pro gear’ casting—anti‑elitist by design
• No all‑white, all‑20‑something ensembles
• No performative influencer poses; keep it conversational and relaxed
• No suggestive or adult-themed zine art in public spaces
• Don’t center lanyards, pins, or props as identity crutches—people first
Community Core Promise
Meet fellow film people in passing, trade one tiny keepsake, and still keep your day wide open.

Participation Style
Entirely drop‑in: arrive during a loose window, make a single swap or quick portrait, or just say hi and drift on—no RSVP, no list, no set times or hosts.

Social Gravity
Little cues—a stitched cuff mark, a pocket strap, a matte mini‑print—spark rail‑side chats and café smiles without needing a plan.

Copy Framing Rule
Use opt‑in phrasing: ‘If you like,’ ‘Drop by for a minute,’ ‘One quick trade,’ never obligations or schedules. Add privacy: share your own photos; avoid filming others. Recognition in walkways is verbal or a nod and under five seconds. Phone photo sharing happens once seated or at adjacent seating only.

Optional Gatherings
• If you notice a tiny coffee mini‑print/postcard swap at café window seating one afternoon, join for a minute (indoors, seated). If it’s busy, choose a quiet lounge corner nearby.
• Shaded‑promenade pairs later in the day: a quick opt‑in portrait in passing—keep right, keep strolling.
• Indoor‑only micro‑stamping during a seated coffee swap; then tuck it away.
Red-Team Summary
Exceptionally thoughtful, ship‑first, opt‑in brief with strong privacy, safety, and anti‑program guardrails. However, multiple ship‑specific plausibility errors (key venues mislocated or mischaracterized) and inconsistent rail/balcony posture language create conflicts with safety guidance. A small set of typos/template artifacts could also confuse crew. These must be corrected before approval.

block
Approval Recommendation
Hold for revision. Correct the venue mismatches, standardize all rail/balcony posture language, and clean the typos/artifacts; then resubmit for quick re‑review.

Required Fixes
• Replace SC7 Windjammer “open‑air terrace (aft)” with a confirmed Rhapsody‑accurate venue and update all related text/prompts/references.
• Verify Café Latte‑tudes sightlines; if no ocean view, retarget all coffee‑swap visuals to a verified ocean‑window venue and remove “curved hull/ocean through glass” language tied to Latte‑tudes (SC2, LS2, LS6, related storyboards).
• Resolve SC6’s environment mismatch by correcting sports‑deck details or relocating the pass‑by beat to Deck 5 promenade; update prompts accordingly.
• Unify rail/balcony safety language by replacing all “lean/leans into” phrasing with “forearms resting lightly, torso upright, hands inside rail line” wherever rails appear (SC3, LS1, LS4, hero_explainer shot notes).
• Proof and fix typos/duplications/punctuation artifacts across sceneLibrary, storyboards, and countdown notes for a clean production handoff.
Issues
blocker
cruise implausibility
Windjammer “open‑air terrace (aft)” likely not accurate for Rhapsody
productionBible.sceneLibrary.SC7: "Windjammer Café open‑air terrace (aft, dining area)" with terrace rail and outdoor breeze described.

Retarget SC7 to a verified Rhapsody location with similar light (e.g., Windjammer indoor window seating, Solarium/Schooner Bar windows, shaded pool‑deck seating under overhang). Update location, environment details, prompts, and any storyboard/still references that imply an open‑air Windjammer terrace.

blocker
cruise implausibility
Café Latte‑tudes depicted with ocean‑facing curved glass is likely inaccurate
SC2/LS2/LS6 label “Café Latte‑tudes window seating” and describe “curved ship‑hull window glass” with “ocean drift through glass,” while also noting Centrum adjacency and aisle buffers.

Site‑verify Latte‑tudes sightlines. If it lacks ocean windows on Rhapsody, move coffee‑swap visuals to a confirmed ocean‑window venue (e.g., Schooner Bar, Viking Crown Lounge/daytime window seating, or another verified deck‑side window area). Replace all “curved hull/ocean through glass” language tied to Latte‑tudes.

warning
cruise implausibility
Jogging track scene includes mismatched “lifeboats above” detail
SC6 environmentDetails: “lifeboats above, rail openings to sea” while the location is the “Sports deck jogging track.”

Adjust SC6 to match the sports deck realities (pool/sundeck sightlines, no lifeboats overhead) or relocate the passing‑nod beat to Deck 5 promenade where lifeboats above is plausible. Update description and prompts accordingly.

warning
motion safety
“Lean” phrasing conflicts with strict rail/balcony posture rules
Examples: SC3 imagePrompt “a traveler leans into the view”; hero_explainer shot 1 subjectMotion “relaxed lean”; LS1 subjectAction “leaning into the breeze”; LS4 “lean shoulder‑to‑shoulder toward the wake.” Elsewhere, rules require upright torso, hands inside rail line.

Standardize all rail/balcony mentions to: “forearms resting lightly, torso upright, hands inside rail line.” Replace all “lean/leans into” phrasing in SC3, LS1, LS4, and hero_explainer notes. Add an explicit balcony‑posture line mirroring deck‑rail safety in visual.compositionNotes.

warning
production feasibility
Typos/template artifacts that could cause on‑set confusion
SC8 location: “Oceanview stateroom stateroom desk with ocean‑view ” (duplication/trailing space); hero_explainer shot 4: “sslow handheld…”; TikTok storyboard shot 3: “jogging gimbal follow curve”; countdownSeries visual notes contain double periods and stray commas.

Proof and correct all duplicated words, misspellings, and punctuation artifacts across sceneLibrary, storyboards, and countdown notes prior to handoff.

warning
copy alignment
Ringer tee text risks signage/ops framing in hero contexts
Merch niche item: Unisex Ringer Tee front text: “Keep Right, Single‑File.”

Do not feature this tee in hero/CTA placements or as a readable close‑up. If shown, keep incidental and pair with opt‑in ambient copy to avoid instructional/queue implications.

        "narrationScript": "Pack light. Bring a tiny keepsake if you like. There’s always shade, breeze, and time for one quick portrait. Two Tiny Windows, 35mm at Sea. Tell a film friend.",
        "musicDirection": "Minimal lo‑fi with airy pads; a small lift by the final line.",

    ],
    "globalDirectionNotes": "Operationally believable, vacation‑first moments on Rhapsody of the Seas. Keep it drop‑in and human: tiny coffee swap indoors at Café Latte‑tudes with aisles clear; shaded rail moments on Deck 5 starboard only; no stamping outdoors; no lines, no furniture moves. Niche identity stays pocket‑sized—wardrobe stitch, slim strap, tiny zine—never a gear parade. People appear as relaxed solos or pairs; natural diversity across ages and styles. The ocean and ship architecture lead every frame; humans are calm accents. Off‑ship Nassau shot keeps the ship clearly in frame to avoid resort generic. No tracks, cranes, sliders, or floor cables in passenger walkways. Use handheld or compact gimbal only. No exchanges, greetings, or handoffs on gangways or in primary embark/disembark flow paths. Passenger-area capture rules: max two-person crew, one off-frame spotter, off-peak capture only, maintain single-file keep-right flow, and stand down immediately if passenger traffic builds or flow is impeded.",

🧪 Test Lab
Interactive Agent Dev
🎨 Aesthetic Design — Phase 1
Load an existing brief, generate a new one, approve, or delete. Run against a campaign slug.

Campaign Slug
film-and-zine-afloat-2026
Load
Revision deadlock after 2 cycles. The same class of issues survived multiple revisions. Operator intervention required. Surviving fixes: Replace SC7 Windjammer “open‑air terrace (aft)” with a confirmed Rhapsody‑accurate venue and update all related text/prompts/references.; Verify Café Latte‑tudes sightlines; if no ocean view, retarget all coffee‑swap visuals to a verified ocean‑window venue and remove “curved hull/ocean through glass” language tied to Latte‑tudes (SC2, LS2, LS6, related storyboards).; Resolve SC6’s environment mismatch by correcting sports‑deck details or relocating the pass‑by beat to Deck 5 promenade; update prompts accordingly.; Unify rail/balcony safety language by replacing all “lean/leans into” phrasing with “forearms resting lightly, torso upright, hands inside rail line” wherever rails appear (SC3, LS1, LS4, hero_explainer shot notes).; Proof and fix typos/duplications/punctuation artifacts across sceneLibrary, storyboards, and countdown notes for a clean production handoff.
Deterministic Fix Available
These structural issues can be fixed without re-running the LLM. Select the fixes to apply, preview, then apply.

Identity Summary
revision #2

red-team block
Color Palette

#1F2329
Hero Slogan
Two Tiny Windows, 35mm at Sea

Point‑and‑shoot ease. One swap. Sail on.

Voice Persona
A calm print lover who knows good window light and respects the sea.

Casting Goal
Across the set: relaxed pairs and solos first, with a few small trios—people in sea-day ease who happen to love film and small print swaps.

Age Range Guidance
Core 25–45 with meaningful presence of 46–65; occasional early‑20s and 65+ to keep it real to cruise life.

Diversity Intent
Visible mix of skin tones and backgrounds (Black, Asian, Latine, White, mixed). Include varied body types, hairstyles, and personal style. If head coverings or cultural dress appear, treat them as natural wardrobe—not theme markers.

Pairing Guidance
Vary pairings: two friends, mixed‑age duo, queer couple, solo traveler at the rail, small trio at a café. Avoid repeating one default couple profile across shots.

Anti-Stereotype Rules
• No retro-costume cosplay to signal ‘film’
• No gatekeeper ‘pro gear’ casting—anti‑elitist by design
• No all‑white, all‑20‑something ensembles
• No performative influencer poses; keep it conversational and relaxed
• No suggestive or adult-themed zine art in public spaces
• Don’t center lanyards, pins, or props as identity crutches—people first
Community Core Promise
Meet fellow film people in passing, trade one tiny keepsake, and still keep your day wide open.

Participation Style
Entirely drop‑in: arrive during a loose window, make a single swap or quick portrait, or just say hi and drift on—no RSVP, no list, no set times or hosts.

Social Gravity
Little cues—a stitched cuff mark, a pocket strap, a matte mini‑print—spark rail‑side chats and café smiles without needing a plan.

Copy Framing Rule
Use opt‑in phrasing: ‘If you like,’ ‘Drop by for a minute,’ ‘One quick trade,’ never obligations or schedules. Add privacy: share your own photos; avoid filming others. Recognition in walkways is verbal or a nod and under five seconds. Phone photo sharing happens once seated or at adjacent seating only.

Optional Gatherings
• If you notice a tiny coffee mini‑print/postcard swap at café window seating one afternoon, join for a minute (indoors, seated). If it’s busy, choose a quiet lounge corner nearby.
• Shaded‑promenade pairs later in the day: a quick opt‑in portrait in passing—keep right, keep strolling.
• Indoor‑only micro‑stamping during a seated coffee swap; then tuck it away.
Red-Team Summary
Exceptionally thoughtful, ship‑first, opt‑in brief with strong privacy, safety, and anti‑program guardrails. However, multiple ship‑specific plausibility errors (key venues mislocated or mischaracterized) and inconsistent rail/balcony posture language create conflicts with safety guidance. A small set of typos/template artifacts could also confuse crew. These must be corrected before approval.

block
Approval Recommendation
Hold for revision. Correct the venue mismatches, standardize all rail/balcony posture language, and clean the typos/artifacts; then resubmit for quick re‑review.

Required Fixes
• Replace SC7 Windjammer “open‑air terrace (aft)” with a confirmed Rhapsody‑accurate venue and update all related text/prompts/references.
• Verify Café Latte‑tudes sightlines; if no ocean view, retarget all coffee‑swap visuals to a verified ocean‑window venue and remove “curved hull/ocean through glass” language tied to Latte‑tudes (SC2, LS2, LS6, related storyboards).
• Resolve SC6’s environment mismatch by correcting sports‑deck details or relocating the pass‑by beat to Deck 5 promenade; update prompts accordingly.
• Unify rail/balcony safety language by replacing all “lean/leans into” phrasing with “forearms resting lightly, torso upright, hands inside rail line” wherever rails appear (SC3, LS1, LS4, hero_explainer shot notes).
• Proof and fix typos/duplications/punctuation artifacts across sceneLibrary, storyboards, and countdown notes for a clean production handoff.
Issues
blocker
cruise implausibility
Windjammer “open‑air terrace (aft)” likely not accurate for Rhapsody
productionBible.sceneLibrary.SC7: "Windjammer Café open‑air terrace (aft, dining area)" with terrace rail and outdoor breeze described.

Retarget SC7 to a verified Rhapsody location with similar light (e.g., Windjammer indoor window seating, Solarium/Schooner Bar windows, shaded pool‑deck seating under overhang). Update location, environment details, prompts, and any storyboard/still references that imply an open‑air Windjammer terrace.

blocker
cruise implausibility
Café Latte‑tudes depicted with ocean‑facing curved glass is likely inaccurate
SC2/LS2/LS6 label “Café Latte‑tudes window seating” and describe “curved ship‑hull window glass” with “ocean drift through glass,” while also noting Centrum adjacency and aisle buffers.

Site‑verify Latte‑tudes sightlines. If it lacks ocean windows on Rhapsody, move coffee‑swap visuals to a confirmed ocean‑window venue (e.g., Schooner Bar, Viking Crown Lounge/daytime window seating, or another verified deck‑side window area). Replace all “curved hull/ocean through glass” language tied to Latte‑tudes.

warning
cruise implausibility
Jogging track scene includes mismatched “lifeboats above” detail
SC6 environmentDetails: “lifeboats above, rail openings to sea” while the location is the “Sports deck jogging track.”

Adjust SC6 to match the sports deck realities (pool/sundeck sightlines, no lifeboats overhead) or relocate the passing‑nod beat to Deck 5 promenade where lifeboats above is plausible. Update description and prompts accordingly.

warning
motion safety
“Lean” phrasing conflicts with strict rail/balcony posture rules
Examples: SC3 imagePrompt “a traveler leans into the view”; hero_explainer shot 1 subjectMotion “relaxed lean”; LS1 subjectAction “leaning into the breeze”; LS4 “lean shoulder‑to‑shoulder toward the wake.” Elsewhere, rules require upright torso, hands inside rail line.

Standardize all rail/balcony mentions to: “forearms resting lightly, torso upright, hands inside rail line.” Replace all “lean/leans into” phrasing in SC3, LS1, LS4, and hero_explainer notes. Add an explicit balcony‑posture line mirroring deck‑rail safety in visual.compositionNotes.

warning
production feasibility
Typos/template artifacts that could cause on‑set confusion
SC8 location: “Oceanview stateroom stateroom desk with ocean‑view ” (duplication/trailing space); hero_explainer shot 4: “sslow handheld…”; TikTok storyboard shot 3: “jogging gimbal follow curve”; countdownSeries visual notes contain double periods and stray commas.

Proof and correct all duplicated words, misspellings, and punctuation artifacts across sceneLibrary, storyboards, and countdown notes prior to handoff.

warning
copy alignment
Ringer tee text risks signage/ops framing in hero contexts
Merch niche item: Unisex Ringer Tee front text: “Keep Right, Single‑File.”

Do not feature this tee in hero/CTA placements or as a readable close‑up. If shown, keep incidental and pair with opt‑in ambient copy to avoid instructional/queue implications.

    "globalDirectionNotes": "Operationally believable, vacation‑first moments on Rhapsody of the Seas. Keep it drop‑in and human: tiny coffee swap indoors at Café Latte‑tudes with aisles clear; shaded rail moments on Deck 5 starboard only; no stamping outdoors; no lines, no furniture moves. Niche identity stays pocket‑sized—wardrobe stitch, slim strap, tiny zine—never a gear parade. People appear as relaxed solos or pairs; natural diversity across ages and styles. The ocean and ship architecture lead every frame; humans are calm accents. Off‑ship Nassau shot keeps the ship clearly in frame to avoid resort generic. No tracks, cranes, sliders, or floor cables in passenger walkways. Use handheld or compact gimbal only. No exchanges, greetings, or handoffs on gangways or in primary embark/disembark flow paths. Passenger-area capture rules: max two-person crew, one off-frame spotter, off-peak capture only, maintain single-file keep-right flow, and stand down immediately if passenger traffic builds or flow is impeded.",

🧪 Test Lab
Interactive Agent Dev
🎨 Aesthetic Design — Phase 1
Load an existing brief, generate a new one, approve, or delete. Run against a campaign slug.

Campaign Slug
film-and-zine-afloat-2026
Load
Selected deterministic fixes were already satisfied in the current brief. The stale fix panel was cleared. Re-run Red Team or Revise to generate fresh suggestions.
Actions
Re-generate
Re-gen Bible
Re-run Red Team
Revise
Approve
Delete Brief
Identity Summary
revision #2
revised
red-team block
Color Palette
primary
#0E2A47
secondary
#9CCFC6
accent
#E96E5A
background
#F7F3EA
textOnDark
#F2F0E6
textOnLight
#1F2329
Hero Slogan
Two Tiny Windows, 35mm at Sea

Point‑and‑shoot ease. One swap. Sail on.

Voice Persona
A calm print lover who knows good window light and respects the sea.

Casting Goal
Across the set: relaxed pairs and solos first, with a few small trios—people in sea-day ease who happen to love film and small print swaps.

Age Range Guidance
Core 25–45 with meaningful presence of 46–65; occasional early‑20s and 65+ to keep it real to cruise life.

Diversity Intent
Visible mix of skin tones and backgrounds (Black, Asian, Latine, White, mixed). Include varied body types, hairstyles, and personal style. If head coverings or cultural dress appear, treat them as natural wardrobe—not theme markers.

Pairing Guidance
Vary pairings: two friends, mixed‑age duo, queer couple, solo traveler at the rail, small trio at a café. Avoid repeating one default couple profile across shots.

Anti-Stereotype Rules
• No retro-costume cosplay to signal ‘film’
• No gatekeeper ‘pro gear’ casting—anti‑elitist by design
• No all‑white, all‑20‑something ensembles
• No performative influencer poses; keep it conversational and relaxed
• No suggestive or adult-themed zine art in public spaces
• Don’t center lanyards, pins, or props as identity crutches—people first
Community Core Promise
Meet fellow film people in passing, trade one tiny keepsake, and still keep your day wide open.

Participation Style
Entirely drop‑in: arrive during a loose window, make a single swap or quick portrait, or just say hi and drift on—no RSVP, no list, no set times or hosts.

Social Gravity
Little cues—a stitched cuff mark, a pocket strap, a matte mini‑print—spark rail‑side chats and café smiles without needing a plan.

Copy Framing Rule
Use opt‑in phrasing: ‘If you like,’ ‘Drop by for a minute,’ ‘One quick trade,’ never obligations or schedules. Add privacy: share your own photos; avoid filming others. Recognition in walkways is verbal or a nod and under five seconds. Phone photo sharing happens once seated or at adjacent seating only.

Optional Gatherings
• If you notice a tiny coffee mini‑print/postcard swap at café window seating one afternoon, join for a minute (indoors, seated). If it’s busy, choose a quiet lounge corner nearby.
• Shaded‑promenade pairs later in the day: a quick opt‑in portrait in passing—keep right, keep strolling.
• Indoor‑only micro‑stamping during a seated coffee swap; then tuck it away.
Red-Team Summary
Exceptionally thoughtful, ship‑first, opt‑in brief with strong privacy, safety, and anti‑program guardrails. However, multiple ship‑specific plausibility errors (key venues mislocated or mischaracterized) and inconsistent rail/balcony posture language create conflicts with safety guidance. A small set of typos/template artifacts could also confuse crew. These must be corrected before approval.

block
Approval Recommendation
Hold for revision. Correct the venue mismatches, standardize all rail/balcony posture language, and clean the typos/artifacts; then resubmit for quick re‑review.

Required Fixes
• Replace SC7 Windjammer “open‑air terrace (aft)” with a confirmed Rhapsody‑accurate venue and update all related text/prompts/references.
• Verify Café Latte‑tudes sightlines; if no ocean view, retarget all coffee‑swap visuals to a verified ocean‑window venue and remove “curved hull/ocean through glass” language tied to Latte‑tudes (SC2, LS2, LS6, related storyboards).
• Resolve SC6’s environment mismatch by correcting sports‑deck details or relocating the pass‑by beat to Deck 5 promenade; update prompts accordingly.
• Unify rail/balcony safety language by replacing all “lean/leans into” phrasing with “forearms resting lightly, torso upright, hands inside rail line” wherever rails appear (SC3, LS1, LS4, hero_explainer shot notes).
• Proof and fix typos/duplications/punctuation artifacts across sceneLibrary, storyboards, and countdown notes for a clean production handoff.
Issues
blocker
cruise implausibility
Windjammer “open‑air terrace (aft)” likely not accurate for Rhapsody
productionBible.sceneLibrary.SC7: "Windjammer Café open‑air terrace (aft, dining area)" with terrace rail and outdoor breeze described.

Retarget SC7 to a verified Rhapsody location with similar light (e.g., Windjammer indoor window seating, Solarium/Schooner Bar windows, shaded pool‑deck seating under overhang). Update location, environment details, prompts, and any storyboard/still references that imply an open‑air Windjammer terrace.

blocker
cruise implausibility
Café Latte‑tudes depicted with ocean‑facing curved glass is likely inaccurate
SC2/LS2/LS6 label “Café Latte‑tudes window seating” and describe “curved ship‑hull window glass” with “ocean drift through glass,” while also noting Centrum adjacency and aisle buffers.

Site‑verify Latte‑tudes sightlines. If it lacks ocean windows on Rhapsody, move coffee‑swap visuals to a confirmed ocean‑window venue (e.g., Schooner Bar, Viking Crown Lounge/daytime window seating, or another verified deck‑side window area). Replace all “curved hull/ocean through glass” language tied to Latte‑tudes.

warning
cruise implausibility
Jogging track scene includes mismatched “lifeboats above” detail
SC6 environmentDetails: “lifeboats above, rail openings to sea” while the location is the “Sports deck jogging track.”

Adjust SC6 to match the sports deck realities (pool/sundeck sightlines, no lifeboats overhead) or relocate the passing‑nod beat to Deck 5 promenade where lifeboats above is plausible. Update description and prompts accordingly.

warning
motion safety
“Lean” phrasing conflicts with strict rail/balcony posture rules
Examples: SC3 imagePrompt “a traveler leans into the view”; hero_explainer shot 1 subjectMotion “relaxed lean”; LS1 subjectAction “leaning into the breeze”; LS4 “lean shoulder‑to‑shoulder toward the wake.” Elsewhere, rules require upright torso, hands inside rail line.

Standardize all rail/balcony mentions to: “forearms resting lightly, torso upright, hands inside rail line.” Replace all “lean/leans into” phrasing in SC3, LS1, LS4, and hero_explainer notes. Add an explicit balcony‑posture line mirroring deck‑rail safety in visual.compositionNotes.

warning
production feasibility
Typos/template artifacts that could cause on‑set confusion
SC8 location: “Oceanview stateroom stateroom desk with ocean‑view ” (duplication/trailing space); hero_explainer shot 4: “sslow handheld…”; TikTok storyboard shot 3: “jogging gimbal follow curve”; countdownSeries visual notes contain double periods and stray commas.

Proof and correct all duplicated words, misspellings, and punctuation artifacts across sceneLibrary, storyboards, and countdown notes prior to handoff.

warning
copy alignment
Ringer tee text risks signage/ops framing in hero contexts
Merch niche item: Unisex Ringer Tee front text: “Keep Right, Single‑File.”

Do not feature this tee in hero/CTA placements or as a readable close‑up. If shown, keep incidental and pair with opt‑in ambient copy to avoid instructional/queue implications.

    ],
    "globalDirectionNotes": "Operationally believable, vacation‑first moments on Rhapsody of the Seas. Keep it drop‑in and human: tiny coffee swap indoors at Café Latte‑tudes with aisles clear; shaded rail moments on Deck 5 starboard only; no stamping outdoors; no lines, no furniture moves. Niche identity stays pocket‑sized—wardrobe stitch, slim strap, tiny zine—never a gear parade. People appear as relaxed solos or pairs; natural diversity across ages and styles. The ocean and ship architecture lead every frame; humans are calm accents. Off‑ship Nassau shot keeps the ship clearly in frame to avoid resort generic. No tracks, cranes, sliders, or floor cables in passenger walkways. Use handheld or compact gimbal only. No exchanges, greetings, or handoffs on gangways or in primary embark/disembark flow paths. Passenger-area capture rules: max two-person crew, one off-frame spotter, off-peak capture only, maintain single-file keep-right flow, and stand down immediately if passenger traffic builds or flow is impeded.",

🧪 Test Lab
Interactive Agent Dev
🎨 Aesthetic Design — Phase 1
Load an existing brief, generate a new one, approve, or delete. Run against a campaign slug.

Campaign Slug
film-and-zine-afloat-2026
Load
Revision deadlock after 2 cycles. The same class of issues survived multiple revisions. Operator intervention required. Surviving fixes: Replace SC7 Windjammer “open‑air terrace (aft)” with a confirmed Rhapsody‑accurate venue and update all related text/prompts/references.; Verify Café Latte‑tudes sightlines; if no ocean view, retarget all coffee‑swap visuals to a verified ocean‑window venue and remove “curved hull/ocean through glass” language tied to Latte‑tudes (SC2, LS2, LS6, related storyboards).; Resolve SC6’s environment mismatch by correcting sports‑deck details or relocating the pass‑by beat to Deck 5 promenade; update prompts accordingly.; Unify rail/balcony safety language by replacing all “lean/leans into” phrasing with “forearms resting lightly, torso upright, hands inside rail line” wherever rails appear (SC3, LS1, LS4, hero_explainer shot notes).; Proof and fix typos/duplications/punctuation artifacts across sceneLibrary, storyboards, and countdown notes for a clean production handoff.
Deterministic Fix Available
These structural issues can be fixed without re-running the LLM. Select the fixes to apply, preview, then apply.

Identity Summary
revision #2
revised
red-team block
Color Palette
primary

#1F2329
Hero Slogan
Two Tiny Windows, 35mm at Sea

Point‑and‑shoot ease. One swap. Sail on.

Voice Persona
A calm print lover who knows good window light and respects the sea.

Casting Goal
Across the set: relaxed pairs and solos first, with a few small trios—people in sea-day ease who happen to love film and small print swaps.

Age Range Guidance
Core 25–45 with meaningful presence of 46–65; occasional early‑20s and 65+ to keep it real to cruise life.

Diversity Intent
Visible mix of skin tones and backgrounds (Black, Asian, Latine, White, mixed). Include varied body types, hairstyles, and personal style. If head coverings or cultural dress appear, treat them as natural wardrobe—not theme markers.

Pairing Guidance
Vary pairings: two friends, mixed‑age duo, queer couple, solo traveler at the rail, small trio at a café. Avoid repeating one default couple profile across shots.

Anti-Stereotype Rules
• No retro-costume cosplay to signal ‘film’
• No gatekeeper ‘pro gear’ casting—anti‑elitist by design
• No all‑white, all‑20‑something ensembles
• No performative influencer poses; keep it conversational and relaxed
• No suggestive or adult-themed zine art in public spaces
• Don’t center lanyards, pins, or props as identity crutches—people first
Community Core Promise
Meet fellow film people in passing, trade one tiny keepsake, and still keep your day wide open.

Participation Style
Entirely drop‑in: arrive during a loose window, make a single swap or quick portrait, or just say hi and drift on—no RSVP, no list, no set times or hosts.

Social Gravity
Little cues—a stitched cuff mark, a pocket strap, a matte mini‑print—spark rail‑side chats and café smiles without needing a plan.

Copy Framing Rule
Use opt‑in phrasing: ‘If you like,’ ‘Drop by for a minute,’ ‘One quick trade,’ never obligations or schedules. Add privacy: share your own photos; avoid filming others. Recognition in walkways is verbal or a nod and under five seconds. Phone photo sharing happens once seated or at adjacent seating only.

Optional Gatherings
• If you notice a tiny coffee mini‑print/postcard swap at café window seating one afternoon, join for a minute (indoors, seated). If it’s busy, choose a quiet lounge corner nearby.
• Shaded‑promenade pairs later in the day: a quick opt‑in portrait in passing—keep right, keep strolling.
• Indoor‑only micro‑stamping during a seated coffee swap; then tuck it away.
Red-Team Summary
Exceptionally thoughtful, ship‑first, opt‑in brief with strong privacy, safety, and anti‑program guardrails. However, multiple ship‑specific plausibility errors (key venues mislocated or mischaracterized) and inconsistent rail/balcony posture language create conflicts with safety guidance. A small set of typos/template artifacts could also confuse crew. These must be corrected before approval.

block
Approval Recommendation
Hold for revision. Correct the venue mismatches, standardize all rail/balcony posture language, and clean the typos/artifacts; then resubmit for quick re‑review.

Required Fixes
• Replace SC7 Windjammer “open‑air terrace (aft)” with a confirmed Rhapsody‑accurate venue and update all related text/prompts/references.
• Verify Café Latte‑tudes sightlines; if no ocean view, retarget all coffee‑swap visuals to a verified ocean‑window venue and remove “curved hull/ocean through glass” language tied to Latte‑tudes (SC2, LS2, LS6, related storyboards).
• Resolve SC6’s environment mismatch by correcting sports‑deck details or relocating the pass‑by beat to Deck 5 promenade; update prompts accordingly.
• Unify rail/balcony safety language by replacing all “lean/leans into” phrasing with “forearms resting lightly, torso upright, hands inside rail line” wherever rails appear (SC3, LS1, LS4, hero_explainer shot notes).
• Proof and fix typos/duplications/punctuation artifacts across sceneLibrary, storyboards, and countdown notes for a clean production handoff.
Issues
blocker
cruise implausibility
Windjammer “open‑air terrace (aft)” likely not accurate for Rhapsody
productionBible.sceneLibrary.SC7: "Windjammer Café open‑air terrace (aft, dining area)" with terrace rail and outdoor breeze described.

Retarget SC7 to a verified Rhapsody location with similar light (e.g., Windjammer indoor window seating, Solarium/Schooner Bar windows, shaded pool‑deck seating under overhang). Update location, environment details, prompts, and any storyboard/still references that imply an open‑air Windjammer terrace.

blocker
cruise implausibility
Café Latte‑tudes depicted with ocean‑facing curved glass is likely inaccurate
SC2/LS2/LS6 label “Café Latte‑tudes window seating” and describe “curved ship‑hull window glass” with “ocean drift through glass,” while also noting Centrum adjacency and aisle buffers.

Site‑verify Latte‑tudes sightlines. If it lacks ocean windows on Rhapsody, move coffee‑swap visuals to a confirmed ocean‑window venue (e.g., Schooner Bar, Viking Crown Lounge/daytime window seating, or another verified deck‑side window area). Replace all “curved hull/ocean through glass” language tied to Latte‑tudes.

warning
cruise implausibility
Jogging track scene includes mismatched “lifeboats above” detail
SC6 environmentDetails: “lifeboats above, rail openings to sea” while the location is the “Sports deck jogging track.”

Adjust SC6 to match the sports deck realities (pool/sundeck sightlines, no lifeboats overhead) or relocate the passing‑nod beat to Deck 5 promenade where lifeboats above is plausible. Update description and prompts accordingly.

warning
motion safety
“Lean” phrasing conflicts with strict rail/balcony posture rules
Examples: SC3 imagePrompt “a traveler leans into the view”; hero_explainer shot 1 subjectMotion “relaxed lean”; LS1 subjectAction “leaning into the breeze”; LS4 “lean shoulder‑to‑shoulder toward the wake.” Elsewhere, rules require upright torso, hands inside rail line.

Standardize all rail/balcony mentions to: “forearms resting lightly, torso upright, hands inside rail line.” Replace all “lean/leans into” phrasing in SC3, LS1, LS4, and hero_explainer notes. Add an explicit balcony‑posture line mirroring deck‑rail safety in visual.compositionNotes.

warning
production feasibility
Typos/template artifacts that could cause on‑set confusion
SC8 location: “Oceanview stateroom stateroom desk with ocean‑view ” (duplication/trailing space); hero_explainer shot 4: “sslow handheld…”; TikTok storyboard shot 3: “jogging gimbal follow curve”; countdownSeries visual notes contain double periods and stray commas.

Proof and correct all duplicated words, misspellings, and punctuation artifacts across sceneLibrary, storyboards, and countdown notes prior to handoff.

warning
copy alignment
Ringer tee text risks signage/ops framing in hero contexts
Merch niche item: Unisex Ringer Tee front text: “Keep Right, Single‑File.”

Do not feature this tee in hero/CTA placements or as a readable close‑up. If shown, keep incidental and pair with opt‑in ambient copy to avoid instructional/queue implications.

    "globalDirectionNotes": "Operationally believable, vacation‑first moments on Rhapsody of the Seas. Keep it drop‑in and human: tiny coffee swap indoors at Café Latte‑tudes with aisles clear; shaded rail moments on Deck 5 starboard only; no stamping outdoors; no lines, no furniture moves. Niche identity stays pocket‑sized—wardrobe stitch, slim strap, tiny zine—never a gear parade. People appear as relaxed solos or pairs; natural diversity across ages and styles. The ocean and ship architecture lead every frame; humans are calm accents. Off‑ship Nassau shot keeps the ship clearly in frame to avoid resort generic. No tracks, cranes, sliders, or floor cables in passenger walkways. Use handheld or compact gimbal only. No exchanges, greetings, or handoffs on gangways or in primary embark/disembark flow paths. Passenger-area capture rules: max two-person crew, one off-frame spotter, off-peak capture only, maintain single-file keep-right flow, and stand down immediately if passenger traffic builds or flow is impeded.",

🧪 Test Lab
Interactive Agent Dev
🎨 Aesthetic Design — Phase 1
Load an existing brief, generate a new one, approve, or delete. Run against a campaign slug.

Campaign Slug
film-and-zine-afloat-2026
Load
Selected deterministic fixes were already satisfied in the current brief. The stale fix panel was cleared. Re-run Red Team or Revise to generate fresh suggestions.
Actions
Re-generate
Re-gen Bible
Re-run Red Team
Revise
Approve
Delete Brief
Identity Summary
revision #2
revised
red-team block
Color Palette
primary
#0E2A47
secondary
#9CCFC6
accent
#E96E5A
background
#F7F3EA
textOnDark
#F2F0E6
textOnLight
#1F2329
Hero Slogan
Two Tiny Windows, 35mm at Sea

Point‑and‑shoot ease. One swap. Sail on.

Voice Persona
A calm print lover who knows good window light and respects the sea.

Casting Goal
Across the set: relaxed pairs and solos first, with a few small trios—people in sea-day ease who happen to love film and small print swaps.

Age Range Guidance
Core 25–45 with meaningful presence of 46–65; occasional early‑20s and 65+ to keep it real to cruise life.

Diversity Intent
Visible mix of skin tones and backgrounds (Black, Asian, Latine, White, mixed). Include varied body types, hairstyles, and personal style. If head coverings or cultural dress appear, treat them as natural wardrobe—not theme markers.

Pairing Guidance
Vary pairings: two friends, mixed‑age duo, queer couple, solo traveler at the rail, small trio at a café. Avoid repeating one default couple profile across shots.

Anti-Stereotype Rules
• No retro-costume cosplay to signal ‘film’
• No gatekeeper ‘pro gear’ casting—anti‑elitist by design
• No all‑white, all‑20‑something ensembles
• No performative influencer poses; keep it conversational and relaxed
• No suggestive or adult-themed zine art in public spaces
• Don’t center lanyards, pins, or props as identity crutches—people first
Community Core Promise
Meet fellow film people in passing, trade one tiny keepsake, and still keep your day wide open.

Participation Style
Entirely drop‑in: arrive during a loose window, make a single swap or quick portrait, or just say hi and drift on—no RSVP, no list, no set times or hosts.

Social Gravity
Little cues—a stitched cuff mark, a pocket strap, a matte mini‑print—spark rail‑side chats and café smiles without needing a plan.

Copy Framing Rule
Use opt‑in phrasing: ‘If you like,’ ‘Drop by for a minute,’ ‘One quick trade,’ never obligations or schedules. Add privacy: share your own photos; avoid filming others. Recognition in walkways is verbal or a nod and under five seconds. Phone photo sharing happens once seated or at adjacent seating only.

Optional Gatherings
• If you notice a tiny coffee mini‑print/postcard swap at café window seating one afternoon, join for a minute (indoors, seated). If it’s busy, choose a quiet lounge corner nearby.
• Shaded‑promenade pairs later in the day: a quick opt‑in portrait in passing—keep right, keep strolling.
• Indoor‑only micro‑stamping during a seated coffee swap; then tuck it away.
Red-Team Summary
Exceptionally thoughtful, ship‑first, opt‑in brief with strong privacy, safety, and anti‑program guardrails. However, multiple ship‑specific plausibility errors (key venues mislocated or mischaracterized) and inconsistent rail/balcony posture language create conflicts with safety guidance. A small set of typos/template artifacts could also confuse crew. These must be corrected before approval.

block
Approval Recommendation
Hold for revision. Correct the venue mismatches, standardize all rail/balcony posture language, and clean the typos/artifacts; then resubmit for quick re‑review.

Required Fixes
• Replace SC7 Windjammer “open‑air terrace (aft)” with a confirmed Rhapsody‑accurate venue and update all related text/prompts/references.
• Verify Café Latte‑tudes sightlines; if no ocean view, retarget all coffee‑swap visuals to a verified ocean‑window venue and remove “curved hull/ocean through glass” language tied to Latte‑tudes (SC2, LS2, LS6, related storyboards).
• Resolve SC6’s environment mismatch by correcting sports‑deck details or relocating the pass‑by beat to Deck 5 promenade; update prompts accordingly.
• Unify rail/balcony safety language by replacing all “lean/leans into” phrasing with “forearms resting lightly, torso upright, hands inside rail line” wherever rails appear (SC3, LS1, LS4, hero_explainer shot notes).
• Proof and fix typos/duplications/punctuation artifacts across sceneLibrary, storyboards, and countdown notes for a clean production handoff.
Issues
blocker
cruise implausibility
Windjammer “open‑air terrace (aft)” likely not accurate for Rhapsody
productionBible.sceneLibrary.SC7: "Windjammer Café open‑air terrace (aft, dining area)" with terrace rail and outdoor breeze described.

Retarget SC7 to a verified Rhapsody location with similar light (e.g., Windjammer indoor window seating, Solarium/Schooner Bar windows, shaded pool‑deck seating under overhang). Update location, environment details, prompts, and any storyboard/still references that imply an open‑air Windjammer terrace.

blocker
cruise implausibility
Café Latte‑tudes depicted with ocean‑facing curved glass is likely inaccurate
SC2/LS2/LS6 label “Café Latte‑tudes window seating” and describe “curved ship‑hull window glass” with “ocean drift through glass,” while also noting Centrum adjacency and aisle buffers.

Site‑verify Latte‑tudes sightlines. If it lacks ocean windows on Rhapsody, move coffee‑swap visuals to a confirmed ocean‑window venue (e.g., Schooner Bar, Viking Crown Lounge/daytime window seating, or another verified deck‑side window area). Replace all “curved hull/ocean through glass” language tied to Latte‑tudes.

warning
cruise implausibility
Jogging track scene includes mismatched “lifeboats above” detail
SC6 environmentDetails: “lifeboats above, rail openings to sea” while the location is the “Sports deck jogging track.”

Adjust SC6 to match the sports deck realities (pool/sundeck sightlines, no lifeboats overhead) or relocate the passing‑nod beat to Deck 5 promenade where lifeboats above is plausible. Update description and prompts accordingly.

warning
motion safety
“Lean” phrasing conflicts with strict rail/balcony posture rules
Examples: SC3 imagePrompt “a traveler leans into the view”; hero_explainer shot 1 subjectMotion “relaxed lean”; LS1 subjectAction “leaning into the breeze”; LS4 “lean shoulder‑to‑shoulder toward the wake.” Elsewhere, rules require upright torso, hands inside rail line.

Standardize all rail/balcony mentions to: “forearms resting lightly, torso upright, hands inside rail line.” Replace all “lean/leans into” phrasing in SC3, LS1, LS4, and hero_explainer notes. Add an explicit balcony‑posture line mirroring deck‑rail safety in visual.compositionNotes.

warning
production feasibility
Typos/template artifacts that could cause on‑set confusion
SC8 location: “Oceanview stateroom stateroom desk with ocean‑view ” (duplication/trailing space); hero_explainer shot 4: “sslow handheld…”; TikTok storyboard shot 3: “jogging gimbal follow curve”; countdownSeries visual notes contain double periods and stray commas.

Proof and correct all duplicated words, misspellings, and punctuation artifacts across sceneLibrary, storyboards, and countdown notes prior to handoff.

warning
copy alignment
Ringer tee text risks signage/ops framing in hero contexts
Merch niche item: Unisex Ringer Tee front text: “Keep Right, Single‑File.”

Do not feature this tee in hero/CTA placements or as a readable close‑up. If shown, keep incidental and pair with opt‑in ambient copy to avoid instructional/queue implications.

    ],
    "globalDirectionNotes": "Operationally believable, vacation‑first moments on Rhapsody of the Seas. Keep it drop‑in and human: tiny coffee swap indoors at Café Latte‑tudes with aisles clear; shaded rail moments on Deck 5 starboard only; no stamping outdoors; no lines, no furniture moves. Niche identity stays pocket‑sized—wardrobe stitch, slim strap, tiny zine—never a gear parade. People appear as relaxed solos or pairs; natural diversity across ages and styles. The ocean and ship architecture lead every frame; humans are calm accents. Off‑ship Nassau shot keeps the ship clearly in frame to avoid resort generic. No tracks, cranes, sliders, or floor cables in passenger walkways. Use handheld or compact gimbal only. No exchanges, greetings, or handoffs on gangways or in primary embark/disembark flow paths. Passenger-area capture rules: max two-person crew, one off-frame spotter, off-peak capture only, maintain single-file keep-right flow, and stand down immediately if passenger traffic builds or flow is impeded.",

🧪 Test Lab
Interactive Agent Dev
🎨 Aesthetic Design — Phase 1
Load an existing brief, generate a new one, approve, or delete. Run against a campaign slug.

Campaign Slug
film-and-zine-afloat-2026
Load
Revision deadlock after 2 cycles. The same class of issues survived multiple revisions. Operator intervention required. Surviving fixes: Replace SC7 Windjammer “open‑air terrace (aft)” with a confirmed Rhapsody‑accurate venue and update all related text/prompts/references.; Verify Café Latte‑tudes sightlines; if no ocean view, retarget all coffee‑swap visuals to a verified ocean‑window venue and remove “curved hull/ocean through glass” language tied to Latte‑tudes (SC2, LS2, LS6, related storyboards).; Resolve SC6’s environment mismatch by correcting sports‑deck details or relocating the pass‑by beat to Deck 5 promenade; update prompts accordingly.; Unify rail/balcony safety language by replacing all “lean/leans into” phrasing with “forearms resting lightly, torso upright, hands inside rail line” wherever rails appear (SC3, LS1, LS4, hero_explainer shot notes).; Proof and fix typos/duplications/punctuation artifacts across sceneLibrary, storyboards, and countdown notes for a clean production handoff.
Deterministic Fix Available
These structural issues can be fixed without re-running the LLM. Select the fixes to apply, preview, then apply.

Identity Summary
revision #2
revised
red-team block
Color Palette
primary

#1F2329
Hero Slogan
Two Tiny Windows, 35mm at Sea

Point‑and‑shoot ease. One swap. Sail on.

Voice Persona
A calm print lover who knows good window light and respects the sea.

Casting Goal
Across the set: relaxed pairs and solos first, with a few small trios—people in sea-day ease who happen to love film and small print swaps.

Age Range Guidance
Core 25–45 with meaningful presence of 46–65; occasional early‑20s and 65+ to keep it real to cruise life.

Diversity Intent
Visible mix of skin tones and backgrounds (Black, Asian, Latine, White, mixed). Include varied body types, hairstyles, and personal style. If head coverings or cultural dress appear, treat them as natural wardrobe—not theme markers.

Pairing Guidance
Vary pairings: two friends, mixed‑age duo, queer couple, solo traveler at the rail, small trio at a café. Avoid repeating one default couple profile across shots.

Anti-Stereotype Rules
• No retro-costume cosplay to signal ‘film’
• No gatekeeper ‘pro gear’ casting—anti‑elitist by design
• No all‑white, all‑20‑something ensembles
• No performative influencer poses; keep it conversational and relaxed
• No suggestive or adult-themed zine art in public spaces
• Don’t center lanyards, pins, or props as identity crutches—people first
Community Core Promise
Meet fellow film people in passing, trade one tiny keepsake, and still keep your day wide open.

Participation Style
Entirely drop‑in: arrive during a loose window, make a single swap or quick portrait, or just say hi and drift on—no RSVP, no list, no set times or hosts.

Social Gravity
Little cues—a stitched cuff mark, a pocket strap, a matte mini‑print—spark rail‑side chats and café smiles without needing a plan.

Copy Framing Rule
Use opt‑in phrasing: ‘If you like,’ ‘Drop by for a minute,’ ‘One quick trade,’ never obligations or schedules. Add privacy: share your own photos; avoid filming others. Recognition in walkways is verbal or a nod and under five seconds. Phone photo sharing happens once seated or at adjacent seating only.

Optional Gatherings
• If you notice a tiny coffee mini‑print/postcard swap at café window seating one afternoon, join for a minute (indoors, seated). If it’s busy, choose a quiet lounge corner nearby.
• Shaded‑promenade pairs later in the day: a quick opt‑in portrait in passing—keep right, keep strolling.
• Indoor‑only micro‑stamping during a seated coffee swap; then tuck it away.
Red-Team Summary
Exceptionally thoughtful, ship‑first, opt‑in brief with strong privacy, safety, and anti‑program guardrails. However, multiple ship‑specific plausibility errors (key venues mislocated or mischaracterized) and inconsistent rail/balcony posture language create conflicts with safety guidance. A small set of typos/template artifacts could also confuse crew. These must be corrected before approval.

block
Approval Recommendation
Hold for revision. Correct the venue mismatches, standardize all rail/balcony posture language, and clean the typos/artifacts; then resubmit for quick re‑review.

Required Fixes
• Replace SC7 Windjammer “open‑air terrace (aft)” with a confirmed Rhapsody‑accurate venue and update all related text/prompts/references.
• Verify Café Latte‑tudes sightlines; if no ocean view, retarget all coffee‑swap visuals to a verified ocean‑window venue and remove “curved hull/ocean through glass” language tied to Latte‑tudes (SC2, LS2, LS6, related storyboards).
• Resolve SC6’s environment mismatch by correcting sports‑deck details or relocating the pass‑by beat to Deck 5 promenade; update prompts accordingly.
• Unify rail/balcony safety language by replacing all “lean/leans into” phrasing with “forearms resting lightly, torso upright, hands inside rail line” wherever rails appear (SC3, LS1, LS4, hero_explainer shot notes).
• Proof and fix typos/duplications/punctuation artifacts across sceneLibrary, storyboards, and countdown notes for a clean production handoff.
Issues
blocker
cruise implausibility
Windjammer “open‑air terrace (aft)” likely not accurate for Rhapsody
productionBible.sceneLibrary.SC7: "Windjammer Café open‑air terrace (aft, dining area)" with terrace rail and outdoor breeze described.

Retarget SC7 to a verified Rhapsody location with similar light (e.g., Windjammer indoor window seating, Solarium/Schooner Bar windows, shaded pool‑deck seating under overhang). Update location, environment details, prompts, and any storyboard/still references that imply an open‑air Windjammer terrace.

blocker
cruise implausibility
Café Latte‑tudes depicted with ocean‑facing curved glass is likely inaccurate
SC2/LS2/LS6 label “Café Latte‑tudes window seating” and describe “curved ship‑hull window glass” with “ocean drift through glass,” while also noting Centrum adjacency and aisle buffers.

Site‑verify Latte‑tudes sightlines. If it lacks ocean windows on Rhapsody, move coffee‑swap visuals to a confirmed ocean‑window venue (e.g., Schooner Bar, Viking Crown Lounge/daytime window seating, or another verified deck‑side window area). Replace all “curved hull/ocean through glass” language tied to Latte‑tudes.

warning
cruise implausibility
Jogging track scene includes mismatched “lifeboats above” detail
SC6 environmentDetails: “lifeboats above, rail openings to sea” while the location is the “Sports deck jogging track.”

Adjust SC6 to match the sports deck realities (pool/sundeck sightlines, no lifeboats overhead) or relocate the passing‑nod beat to Deck 5 promenade where lifeboats above is plausible. Update description and prompts accordingly.

warning
motion safety
“Lean” phrasing conflicts with strict rail/balcony posture rules
Examples: SC3 imagePrompt “a traveler leans into the view”; hero_explainer shot 1 subjectMotion “relaxed lean”; LS1 subjectAction “leaning into the breeze”; LS4 “lean shoulder‑to‑shoulder toward the wake.” Elsewhere, rules require upright torso, hands inside rail line.

Standardize all rail/balcony mentions to: “forearms resting lightly, torso upright, hands inside rail line.” Replace all “lean/leans into” phrasing in SC3, LS1, LS4, and hero_explainer notes. Add an explicit balcony‑posture line mirroring deck‑rail safety in visual.compositionNotes.

warning
production feasibility
Typos/template artifacts that could cause on‑set confusion
SC8 location: “Oceanview stateroom stateroom desk with ocean‑view ” (duplication/trailing space); hero_explainer shot 4: “sslow handheld…”; TikTok storyboard shot 3: “jogging gimbal follow curve”; countdownSeries visual notes contain double periods and stray commas.

Proof and correct all duplicated words, misspellings, and punctuation artifacts across sceneLibrary, storyboards, and countdown notes prior to handoff.

warning
copy alignment
Ringer tee text risks signage/ops framing in hero contexts
Merch niche item: Unisex Ringer Tee front text: “Keep Right, Single‑File.”

Do not feature this tee in hero/CTA placements or as a readable close‑up. If shown, keep incidental and pair with opt‑in ambient copy to avoid instructional/queue implications.

    "globalDirectionNotes": "Operationally believable, vacation‑first moments on Rhapsody of the Seas. Keep it drop‑in and human: tiny coffee swap indoors at Café Latte‑tudes with aisles clear; shaded rail moments on Deck 5 starboard only; no stamping outdoors; no lines, no furniture moves. Niche identity stays pocket‑sized—wardrobe stitch, slim strap, tiny zine—never a gear parade. People appear as relaxed solos or pairs; natural diversity across ages and styles. The ocean and ship architecture lead every frame; humans are calm accents. Off‑ship Nassau shot keeps the ship clearly in frame to avoid resort generic. No tracks, cranes, sliders, or floor cables in passenger walkways. Use handheld or compact gimbal only. No exchanges, greetings, or handoffs on gangways or in primary embark/disembark flow paths. Passenger-area capture rules: max two-person crew, one off-frame spotter, off-peak capture only, maintain single-file keep-right flow, and stand down immediately if passenger traffic builds or flow is impeded.",
