🧪 Test Lab
Interactive Agent Dev
🎨 Aesthetic Design — Phase 1
Load an existing brief, generate a new one, approve, or delete. Run against a campaign slug.

Campaign Slug
film-and-zine-afloat-2026
Load
Actions
Re-generate
Re-gen Bible
Re-run Red Team
Revise
Approve
Delete Brief
Identity Summary
revision #2
revised
red-team block
Color Palette
primary
#0E2A47
secondary
#9CCFC6
accent
#E96E5A
background
#F7F3EA
textOnDark
#F2F0E6
textOnLight
#1F2329
Hero Slogan
Two Tiny Windows, 35mm at Sea

Point‑and‑shoot ease. One swap. Sail on.

Voice Persona
A calm print lover who knows good window light and respects the sea.

Casting Goal
Across the set: relaxed pairs and solos first, with a few small trios—people in sea-day ease who happen to love film and small print swaps.

Age Range Guidance
Core 25–45 with meaningful presence of 46–65; occasional early‑20s and 65+ to keep it real to cruise life.

Diversity Intent
Visible mix of skin tones and backgrounds (Black, Asian, Latine, White, mixed). Include varied body types, hairstyles, and personal style. If head coverings or cultural dress appear, treat them as natural wardrobe—not theme markers.

Pairing Guidance
Vary pairings: two friends, mixed‑age duo, queer couple, solo traveler at the rail, small trio at a café. Avoid repeating one default couple profile across shots.

Anti-Stereotype Rules
• No retro-costume cosplay to signal ‘film’
• No gatekeeper ‘pro gear’ casting—anti‑elitist by design
• No all‑white, all‑20‑something ensembles
• No performative influencer poses; keep it conversational and relaxed
• No suggestive or adult-themed zine art in public spaces
• Don’t center lanyards, pins, or props as identity crutches—people first
Community Core Promise
Meet fellow film people in passing, trade one tiny keepsake, and still keep your day wide open.

Participation Style
Entirely drop‑in: arrive during a loose window, make a single swap or quick portrait, or just say hi and drift on—no RSVP, no list, no set times or hosts.

Social Gravity
Little cues—a stitched cuff mark, a pocket strap, a matte mini‑print—spark rail‑side chats and café smiles without needing a plan.

Copy Framing Rule
Use opt‑in phrasing: ‘If you like,’ ‘Drop by for a minute,’ ‘One quick trade,’ never obligations or schedules. Add privacy: share your own photos; avoid filming others. Recognition in walkways is verbal or a nod and under five seconds. Phone photo sharing happens once seated or at adjacent seating only.

Optional Gatherings
• If you notice a tiny coffee mini‑print/postcard swap at café window seating one afternoon, join for a minute (indoors, seated). If it’s busy, choose a quiet lounge corner nearby.
• Shaded‑promenade pairs later in the day: a quick opt‑in portrait in passing—keep right, keep strolling.
• Indoor‑only micro‑stamping during a seated coffee swap; then tuck it away.
Red-Team Summary
Exceptionally ship‑first, opt‑in, and privacy‑forward with solid anti‑program guardrails. However, it still contains ship‑specific plausibility errors (mislocated/mischaracterized venues), repeated rail/balcony “lean” directions that conflict with the safety posture rule, and several typos/wording artifacts that can confuse production. These must be corrected before launch.

block
Approval Recommendation
Hold for revision. Fix the venue inaccuracies, standardize all rail/balcony posture language, and clean the typos/artifacts. Then resubmit for quick re‑review.

Required Fixes
• Replace SC7 Windjammer “open‑air terrace (aft)” with a verified Rhapsody‑accurate venue and update all related text/prompts.
• Verify Café Latte‑tudes sightlines; if no ocean view, retarget all coffee‑swap visuals to a confirmed ocean‑window venue and remove “curved hull/ocean through glass” language tied to Latte‑tudes (SC2, LS2, LS6, related storyboards).
• Standardize all rail/balcony posture language to “forearms resting lightly, torso upright, hands inside rail line,” removing all “lean” directions in SC3, LS1, LS4, and hero_explainer notes; add an explicit balcony‑posture line to visual.compositionNotes.
• Resolve SC6 environment mismatch by correcting sports‑deck details or relocating the pass‑by beat to Deck 5 promenade; update prompts accordingly.
• Proof and fix typos/duplications/punctuation artifacts across sceneLibrary, storyboards, and countdown notes for a clean production handoff.
• Normalize cabin category terminology (e.g., “Ocean View Balcony stateroom” vs “Ocean View stateroom”) consistently across SC3/SC8 and any dependent copy.
Issues
blocker
cruise implausibility
Windjammer “open‑air terrace (aft)” likely not accurate for Rhapsody
productionBible.sceneLibrary.SC7 location: "Windjammer Café open‑air terrace (aft, dining area)" with terrace rail/outdoor breeze described.

Retarget SC7 to a verified Rhapsody location with similar light (e.g., Windjammer indoor window seating, Schooner Bar windows, shaded pool‑deck seating under overhang). Update location, environment details, and prompts; remove any implication of an outdoor Windjammer terrace if not present on Rhapsody.

blocker
cruise implausibility
Café Latte‑tudes depicted with ocean‑facing curved glass is likely inaccurate on Rhapsody
SC2/LS2/LS6 label “Café Latte‑tudes window seating/window bar” and describe “curved ship‑hull window glass” with “ocean drift through glass” while also noting Centrum adjacency.

Site‑verify Latte‑tudes sightlines. If it lacks ocean windows, move the coffee‑swap visuals to a confirmed ocean‑window venue (e.g., Schooner Bar, Viking Crown/upper‑lounge windows). Replace all “curved hull/ocean through glass” language tied to Latte‑tudes (SC2, LS2, LS6, related storyboard notes).

blocker
motion safety
“Lean” directions conflict with rail/balcony safety posture rule
Examples: SC3 imagePrompt “a traveler leans into the view”; videoConcepts.heroExplainer.shotSequence[0].subjectMotion “relaxed lean”; landingStillBible.LS1.subjectAction “leaning into the breeze”; landingStillBible.LS4.subjectAction “lean shoulder‑to‑shoulder toward the wake.” Composition notes elsewhere require upright torso/hands inside rail line.

Standardize all rail/balcony instructions to: “forearms resting lightly, torso upright, hands inside rail line.” Replace all “lean/leans into” phrasing in SC3, LS1, LS4, and hero_explainer notes. Add an explicit balcony‑posture line mirroring deck‑rail safety in visual.compositionNotes.

warning
cruise implausibility
Jogging track scene includes mismatched “lifeboats above” detail
SC6 environmentDetails: “lifeboats above, rail openings to sea” while location is “Sports deck jogging track.”

Adjust SC6 to match top‑deck realities (no lifeboats overhead) or relocate the pass‑by beat to Deck 5 promenade where lifeboats above is plausible. Update environment details and prompts accordingly.

warning
production feasibility
Typos/wording artifacts that can confuse production
SC8 location: “Oceanview stateroom stateroom desk with ocean‑view ” (duplication/trailing space). Hero Explainer shot 4: “sslow handheld…”. TikTok Seed shot 3 cameraMovement: “gentle gimbal drift forward along jogging gimbal follow curve.” Countdown series notes contain double periods/stray commas.

Proof and correct duplicated words, misspellings, and punctuation artifacts across sceneLibrary, storyboards, and countdown notes before handoff.

warning
cruise implausibility
Cabin category wording is inconsistent/ambiguous
SC3 location: “Ocean‑view stateroom balcony” (mixes ‘ocean‑view stateroom’ and ‘balcony’ terminology); earlier SC8 uses “Oceanview stateroom stateroom desk…”.

Standardize to Royal‑accurate categories (e.g., “Ocean View Balcony stateroom” or “Ocean View stateroom” with no balcony). Align all related copy and prompts.

warning
copy alignment
Ringer tee motto risks signage/ops energy in hero contexts
Merch niche item: Unisex Ringer Tee front text: “Keep Right, Single‑File.”

Do not hero or closely feature this tee in ads/CTAs. If included, keep incidental and pair with ambient, opt‑in copy to avoid instructional/queue implications.

warning
other
Flash guidance is slightly inconsistent across sections
lightingStyle: “Avoid noon glare and heavy flash.” avoidList: “... flashes after dusk.” discouragedProps: “off‑camera flash (onscreen).”

Consolidate to a single directive (e.g., “avoid flash entirely for this campaign”) or clearly define when/if small on‑camera fill is acceptable, to remove ambiguity for crew.
